content:
💡 Portpal | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Resolving and preventing 'address already in use' errors by providing developers with a clear, visual, and interactive way to see which processes are using specific network ports and to manage those processes.
✨ Key Features: • Real-Time Port Dashboard • One-Click Process Control (Kill/Restart) • Interactive Network Topology Visualization (D3.js) • System Tray Intelligence • Smart Process and Framework Detection
📖 Summary: PortPal is a native desktop application designed to help developers manage and monitor network ports on their machines. It provides a real-time dashboard to view port usage, identify processes, track network traffic, and visualize network topology, enabling quick resolution of port conflicts and process management.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 SMS2Email | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling remote access to SMS messages by forwarding them to an email account.
✨ Key Features: • Forwards incoming SMS messages to email • Configurable SMTP server settings • Accessible remotely via email
📖 Summary: SMS2Email is an Android application designed to forward incoming SMS messages to a specified email address. It allows users to access their text messages remotely by sending them to an SMTP server.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 js-code-to-svg-flowchart | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Visualizing JavaScript code as flowcharts for learning, documentation, and refactoring.
✨ Key Features: • Converts JavaScript code to SVG flowcharts • Supports ES6 syntax • Multiple abstraction levels for code visualization • Customizable styles and themes • CLI tool for generating SVG files
📖 Summary: js2flowchart is a JavaScript library that converts JavaScript code into SVG flowcharts. It aids in understanding, documenting, and refactoring code by providing visual representations at various abstraction levels.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ swagger-ui | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To automatically generate and display interactive API documentation from an OpenAPI specification, enabling easy exploration and testing of API endpoints.
✨ Key Features: • Generates interactive API documentation from OpenAPI specifications • Allows visualization and interaction with API resources • Supports multiple OpenAPI Specification versions (2.0, 3.x, 3.1.x)
📖 Summary: Swagger UI is a JavaScript-based tool that generates interactive API documentation from OpenAPI specifications. It allows developers and consumers to visualize and interact with API resources without needing to implement the API logic, making API exploration and integration significantly easier.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 expo | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Developing and deploying cross-platform native applications for mobile and web from a single codebase.
✨ Key Features: • Universal native apps for Android, iOS, and web • Built with React and JavaScript • Includes Expo SDK, Modules API, Go app, CLI, Router • Integration with Expo Application Services (EAS) for build, ship, and iterate • Open-source framework
📖 Summary: Expo is an open-source framework for building universal native applications for Android, iOS, and the web using React and JavaScript. It provides a unified runtime and a suite of tools to simplify development, shipping, and iteration, integrating with Expo Application Services (EAS) for enhanced capabilities.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
